'''David Mills''' (born 1 June 1981) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played for the [[Widnes Vikings]], [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] in two separate spells, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Leigh Centurions]],<ref name="They Played For Leigh (Statistics) at leighrl.co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/v2/they-played-for-leigh|title=They Played For Leigh (Statistics) at leighrl.co.uk|publisher=leighrl.co.uk|date=31 December 2019|accessdate=1 January 2020}}</ref><ref name="Heritage Numbers at leighrl.co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/v2/club/heritage-numbers|title=Heritage Numbers at leighrl.co.uk|publisher=leighrl.co.uk|date=31 December 2020|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> [[Swinton Lions]] and the [[North Wales Crusaders]]. He also played for [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in the War of the Roses.

==Background==
Mills was born in [[Widnes]], [[Cheshire]], England, he has [[Welsh people|Welsh]] [[ancestor]]s, and eligible to play for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] due to the [[Grandfather rule|grandparent rule]], as he is the son of the Welsh rugby league footballer; [[Jim Mills (rugby league)|'Big Jim' Mills]].

==Playing career==
He has previously played for his hometown club [[Widnes Vikings]].{{citation needed|date=June 2019}}

He joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] for the 2008 season after they avoided relegation in 2007's [[Super League XII]].<ref name="Quins prop Mills moves to Hull KR">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/7003885.stm|title=Quins prop Mills moves to Hull KR|publisher=BBC|date=2007-09-20|access-date=2007-09-20}}</ref><ref name="Two More Signings For Hull KR">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/09/20/RUGBYL_Hull_KR.html|title=Two More Signings For Hull KR|publisher=BBC|date=2007-09-20|accessdate=2007-09-20|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110604235844/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F07%2F09%2F20%2FRUGBYL_Hull_KR.html|archivedate=2011-06-04}}</ref>

He was named as captain of the [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] squad to face [[England national rugby league team|England]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]] prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Crusaders dominate Wales' squad">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Crusaders dominate Wales' squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-03|access-date=2008-10-04}}</ref>

[[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] and [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] were interested in signing Mills due to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] looking to release him to make way for [[Joel Clinton]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sporthull.co.uk/hullcity/Hull-KR-Mills-make-way-Clinton-arrival/article-1561745-detail/article.html|title=Hull Daily News Online & Hull Events | Hull Daily Mail|website=Sporthull.co.uk|date=|accessdate=2016-08-08}}</ref> however he joined (though never played for) newly formed Welsh club South Wales Scorpions.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleague.com/rugby-league-news/duo-sign-for-scorpions.html|title=Archived copy|accessdate=2010-02-22|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110715214419/http://www.rugbyleague.com/rugby-league-news/duo-sign-for-scorpions.html|archivedate=2011-07-15}}</ref>

On 14 May 2010, he returned to Harlequins in a one-month loan agreement.<ref>[https://archive.today/20120908222019/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=36318 Mills returns to Quins on loan]</ref>

Mills spent the 2011 season with [[Leigh Centurions]], winning the 2011 [[Northern Rail Cup]] in the process before signing for [[Swinton Lions]] ahead of the 2012 season.{{citation needed|date=June 2019}}
